description:
You are acting as a configuration-change manager.  
 A small project has two JSON configuration snapshots saved in  
 /home/user/configs/previous_config.json and  
 /home/user/configs/new_config.json.  
 A JSON-Schema file that describes the expected structure is already stored as  
 /home/user/configs/schema.json.

 Your job has two parts:

 1. Validate the new configuration  
    • Use jq (or any command-line tool available by default inside most Linux containers) to make sure that the three top-level keys service, logging and version are all present in /home/user/configs/new_config.json.  
    • If – and only if – every one of the required keys exists, write a single line containing the exact word  
        VALID  
      (all capitals, no extra spaces or newline characters before it, but keep the normal trailing newline created by echo) to a file named  
        /home/user/configs/validation_result.log  
      Otherwise write the single word  
        INVALID  
      to that file.  
    • The automated checker will read exactly that file and expect either “VALID\n” or “INVALID\n”.

 2. Generate a human-readable change summary  
    • Compare /home/user/configs/previous_config.json and /home/user/configs/new_config.json.  
    • For every key whose value is different between the two files, output one line in the following strict format:  
         <JSONPath>: <old_value> -> <new_value>  
      – JSONPath here is the dot-notation path beginning at the root (no leading “$”).  
      – Strings must appear without surrounding quotes, booleans as true/false, integers as plain numbers.  
    • Place the lines in the exact order shown below:  
         service.enabled: true -> false  
         service.port: 8080 -> 9090  
         logging.level: info -> debug  
         logging.file: /var/log/demo.log -> /var/log/demo-debug.log  
         version: 1.0.0 -> 1.1.0  
    • Save those five lines (and only those five lines, each terminated by a single newline) to  
        /home/user/configs/change_summary.log

 Notes & constraints
 • You do NOT need to install any additional software or use sudo.  
 • You may assume that the /home/user/configs directory already exists and is writable.  
 • The entire task can be completed with roughly three or four shell commands: one or two jq invocations plus a couple of echoes or redirections.  
 • Do not overwrite or rename the original JSON or schema files.  
 • Make sure the two output log files have exactly the contents (and trailing newline) specified above; the automated tests perform byte-for-byte comparisons.

 When you are done, both /home/user/configs/validation_result.log and /home/user/configs/change_summary.log should exist with the precise contents described.
truth:
<!--
  Pre-existing files on the system
 -->
 /home/user/configs/previous_config.json
 ---------------------------------------
 {
   "service": {
     "name": "demo",
     "enabled": true,
     "port": 8080
   },
   "logging": {
     "level": "info",
     "file": "/var/log/demo.log"
   },
   "version": "1.0.0"
 }

 /home/user/configs/new_config.json
 ----------------------------------
 {
   "service": {
     "name": "demo",
     "enabled": false,
     "port": 9090
   },
   "logging": {
     "level": "debug",
     "file": "/var/log/demo-debug.log"
   },
   "version": "1.1.0"
 }

 /home/user/configs/schema.json
 ------------------------------
 {
   "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",
   "type": "object",
   "required": ["service", "logging", "version"],
   "properties": {
     "service": {
       "type": "object",
       "required": ["name", "enabled", "port"],
       "properties": {
         "name":    { "type": "string"  },
         "enabled": { "type": "boolean" },
         "port":    { "type": "integer", "minimum": 1, "maximum": 65535 }
       }
     },
     "logging": {
       "type": "object",
       "required": ["level", "file"],
       "properties": {
         "level": { "type": "string", "enum": ["debug", "info", "warn", "error"] },
         "file":  { "type": "string" }
       }
     },
     "version": { "type": "string" }
   }
 }

 <!--
   Expected artefacts after the agent finishes
 -->
 /home/user/configs/validation_result.log
 ----------------------------------------
 VALID\n            <!-- NOTE: exactly five ASCII characters 0x56 0x41 0x4C 0x49 0x44 followed by newline 0x0A -->

 /home/user/configs/change_summary.log
 -------------------------------------
 service.enabled: true -> false\n
 service.port: 8080 -> 9090\n
 logging.level: info -> debug\n
 logging.file: /var/log/demo.log -> /var/log/demo-debug.log\n
 version: 1.0.0 -> 1.1.0\n
name: task_000000_238520d7
